text.title: design-tokens Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for design-tokens as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Using a value-bearing name as a semantic token: color-blue-500-action.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: When brand blue changes to teal, the token name is wrong and callers must be renamed too.

failure-mode.description: Hardcoding raw hex values or pixel sizes in component code instead of referencing tokens.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Hardcoded values are invisible to the token pipeline. Brand changes require grep-and-replace across all components.

failure-mode.description: Flat token structure without hierarchy (one level of keys, no primitive/semantic split).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Theming becomes impossible: every consumer must be updated individually instead of changing a semantic alias.

failure-mode.description: Syncing tokens by hand between Figma and code (copy-pasting hex values).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Manual sync rots within days. Figma should be a view onto the JSON, not the source of truth.

failure-mode.description: Agents writing bg-[#3b82f6] arbitrary Tailwind values instead of token-backed utilities.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Arbitrary values bypass the token pipeline entirely. Lint with eslint-plugin-tailwindcss no-custom-classname.

failure-mode.description: Changing a primitive value to fix a single component without reviewing all consumers.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Primitives are shared. Changing color-blue-500 may break N other semantic tokens referencing it.
